summary: Analysis of whether UNGA will adopt resolution condemning US Venezuela operation before Feb 1, 2026
content:
Key facts:
- US operation captured Maduro on Jan 3, 2026
- SC emergency meeting Jan 5 - widespread condemnation from allies and adversaries
- Secretary General said US violated UN charter
- France (P5 member) explicitly condemned the action
- As of Jan 11, NO SC resolution has been voted on, NO UNGA resolution drafted

Historical precedents:
- Panama 1989: Invasion Dec 20 → SC veto Dec 23 → UNGA resolution Dec 29 (9 days)
- Grenada 1983: Invasion Oct 25 → SC veto ~3 days → UNGA resolution Nov 2 (8 days)
- Iraq 2003: No UNGA condemnation despite massive opposition
- Ukraine 2022: SC veto Feb 25 → UNGA resolution March 2 (5 days after veto)

Key difference: For Panama, France and UK vetoed WITH the US. For Venezuela, France is CONDEMNING the US - making a SC resolution more likely to pass (US-only veto), strengthening case for UNGA action.

Counter-precedent: Iraq 2003 had a coalition of the willing and legal ambiguity from Resolution 1441. Venezuela case is more like Panama/Grenada.

Concern: 8 days without any formal resolution process is a negative signal. In Panama/Grenada, SC votes happened within 3 days.

20 days remaining until deadline - substantial time for process to play out.
